gates.name: leakage_check
gates.threshold: no shared windows; injured-cohort 30-day pre-IL excluded from training
gates.measured: per-pitcher checkpoints in models/mechanix_ae/per_pitcher/, --skip-train, training excludes 30-day pre-IL (spec section A); ROC separates injured-cohort positives from matched healthy negatives

gates.name: median_lead_time_days
gates.notes: lead_time_t2.0 (z=2.0 breach), n_breached=81/92 (88.0%); t1.0 median=59.5 days, t3.0 median=43.0 days

gates.name: fpr_at_mdi_80
gates.notes: zscore-mode reports pct_breach_z1=100, pct_breach_z2=96, pct_breach_z3=86; legacy MDI-percentile fpr_summary.json gives pct_breach_60/70/80 = 100/100/100

notes: DECISION GATE TRIGGERED: AUC(MDI)=0.387 is below the 0.58 PIVOT threshold AND below random (0.5), implying MDI is anti-correlated with injury under z-score mode. Per spec section D, PIVOT narrative from 'injury early-warning' to 'mechanical profiling'. Lead-time and delta_auc gates both pass, but the FPR gate is catastrophically failed (100% of healthy seasons breach all thresholds in z-score mode), confirming the model in this scoring mode flags every pitcher. Consider re-running with --score-mode percentile to compare; legacy MDI-percentile FPR also reports 100%/100%/100% so the FPR signal is unambiguous. n_healthy_fpr capped at 50 because cohort split allocates 50 healthy seasons to ROC and 50 to FPR (script log).
